reasoning: Response B is significantly stronger across almost all requirements. It properly normalizes the React state (using ID references for cards and columns), which is best practice for Kanban boards and makes updates much more efficient. Response B successfully implements the search/filter feature by visibly dimming non-matching cards, while Response A completely ignores the filtered criteria in its render method. Response B also actually implements the Undo history by wrapping context dispatches; Response A sets up the undo stack but never intercepts or pushes actions to it, leaving it completely non-functional. Furthermore, Response A violated the negative constraint 'Do not use placeholder comments in place of real implementation' by leaving // comments for keyboard accessibility logic. While Response B had a minor hallucination artifact in its keyboard logic (attempting to call an invalid `getState()` on a hook) and a misplaced import at the end of App.tsx, its complete implementation of Undo, functional filtering, screen-reader live regions, and visible drop placeholders heavily outweighs these minor stylistic issues.
